methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Mythic is a Series D-stage semiconductor company that has raised $125M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), with direct peer comparables including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and DEEPX.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implies a valuation of $188M. The peer-median approach references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: SambaNova Systems at $5.0B, Tenstorrent at $2.7B, and DEEPX at $529M. The median of this cohort is $2.7B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 25.3x across the three public peers) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically do not disclose. 

Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $159M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Mythic trades closer to earlier-stage funding-based metrics or to the upper-quartile peer valuations. Key uncertainties include product commercialization progress relative to peers and actual revenue traction, neither of which are available on file.
